title: oldenlandia diffusa
intro: Oldenlandia diffusa, also known as "Phuoc Binh" or "Mukunoki," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Oldenlandia diffusa include its ability to lower fever, reduce inflammation, and improve immune function, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions such as fever, infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be found in traditional Asian medicines in the form of capsules or tablets.
